title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
At the Jolly Coffee Shop, Poirot speaks with Phyllida regarding the disappearance of Jean. Phyllida claims she has been thinking about Jean but cannot remember any specific actions or words that seemed unusual, expressing a cynical view toward worrying about others. Poirot remains focused on finding Jean despite Phyllida's suggestion that his concerns might be imagined.

Later, the narrator brings Samuel Kid, a boiler maker, to the café. Kid is described as having a dirty appearance and a half-shaven face. After initially mocking Poirot's accent, Kid recounts an observation from the previous night. He claims that just after eight o'clock, he saw a terrified woman running from the Brooks Han Hotel. As she ran, she dropped two golden keys, which she later rushed back to retrieve. Kid notes that the keys had numbers on them: one was in the one-hundreds and the other was in the three-hundreds.

This detail prompts the narrator to recall that Richard Nigges's key, found by Poirot, was numbered 238, and notes that Harriet Sipper and Edith Gransberry live at addresses 121 and 317, respectively. When Poirot asks if the woman had light blonde hair, a brown hat, and a brown coat—matching Jenny's description—Kid clarifies that the woman had black, curly hair and wore a light blue hat. The narrator realizes that Kid may have nearly encountered the murderer.
